REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT PATRIOT CAPITAL ADVISORS, LLC'S NOTICE OF RESCHEDULED MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER AWARDING ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS IS DENIED. ASSUMING THAT DEFENDANT IS ABLE TO RECOVER FEES UNDER THE NOTE, DEFENDANT FAILED TO INCLUDE ANY COMPETENT AND ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE AS TO THE REASONABLENESS OF THE HOURS SPENT ON THE ACTIVITIES IN THIS ACTION. WHILE INCLUSION OF THE INVOICES IS NOT MANDATORY, THE DECLARATION SHOULD HAVE AT LEAST INCLUDED THE NUMBER OF HOURS, THE HOURLY RATE AND A GENERAL DESCRIPTION OF THE TASKS PERFORMED, E.G. DEMURRER - 10 HOURS; COURT APPEARANCES - 10 HOURS, ETC. INCLUSION OF SUCH MINIMALLY REQUIRED MOST BASIC INFORMATION (THAT WAS READILY AVAILABLE TO THE MOVING PARTY) WITH THE REPLY IS PROCEDURALLY IMPROPER. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST AS TO COSTS IS GRANTED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT PATRIOT CAPITAL ADVISORS, LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Awarding Attorneys Fees And Costs is continued to April 29, 2021 on Court's own motion. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

Notice Of Rescheduled Motion And Motion For Order Awarding Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Mar-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Steven C. Shuman; Engstrom Lipscomb & Lack; 310-552-3800 ext. 336; appearing via CourtCall on behalf of plaintiff Tatiana Dins.
Robert Anderson; Law Offices of Robert B. Anderson; 415-994-0311; appearing via CourtCall on behalf of defendant Patriot Capital Advisors, LLC.

Parties discuss the court's tentative ruling. Defendant submits to the tentative ruling regarding attorney fees, but requests that the court modify it's ruling to award the requested costs. The court rules as follows: DEFENDANT PATRIOT CAPITAL ADVISORS, LLC's Notice Of Rescheduled Motion And Motion For Order Awarding Attorneys Fees And Costs is DENIED. Assuming that defendant is able to recover fees under the Note, Defendant failed to include any competent and admissible evidence as to the reasonableness of the hours spent on the activities in this action. While inclusion of the invoices is not mandatory, the declaration should have at least included the number of hours, the hourly rate and a general description of the tasks performed, e.g. demurrer - 10 hours; court appearances - 10 hours, etc. Inclusion of such minimally required most basic information (that was readily available to the moving party) with the reply is procedurally improper. Defendant's request as to costs is GRANTED. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
